prompt.prompt_class: STANDARD AI / GITHUB CLI REPOSITORY CREATION
prompt.sprint_path_role: Safely create and verify a new GitHub repository and local checkout through Git and GitHub CLI
prompt.use_this_when: The operator wants to create a new GitHub repository and either clone a new initialized remote locally or publish one explicitly selected existing local repository.
prompt.inspect_first: Requested owner/name, explicit visibility, description, verified local parent or source directory, Git and GitHub CLI availability, active GitHub account, remote-name policy, README/license/gitignore/template choices, repository collision checks, and mutation authority.
prompt.expected_output: A verified GitHub repository, verified local root and origin remote, optional initial files or pushed history exactly as requested, command evidence, repository URL, branch state, and a bounded next command.
prompt.next_step: Enter the verified new root, inspect repository law or create the initial harness, then use P03 or P07 for the first bounded repository sprint.
prompt.proof_gate: The intended parent or source directory is verified first, the owner/name and visibility are explicit, authentication is healthy without exposing a token, no repository collision exists, gh repo create succeeds, and both gh repo view and git remote evidence match the requested repository.
lines: PROMPT SURFACE: STANDARD AI. THIS IS NOT A GOODNIGHT, HAVE FUN (GNHF) TERMINAL COMMAND.
CREATE AND VERIFY A NEW GITHUB REPOSITORY THROUGH GIT AND GITHUB CLI. DO NOT RETURN A PLAN ONLY WHEN THE OPERATOR HAS EXPLICITLY AUTHORIZED CREATION.
Requested repository: xyz_owner/xyz_repo_name
Visibility: xyz_private_public_or_internal
Description: xyz_description
Local mode: xyz_clone_new_remote_or_publish_existing_local_repo
Local parent or source directory: xyz_verified_path_or_unknown
Bootstrap options: xyz_readme_license_gitignore_template_or_none
Branch/worktree context: new repository; no branch may be assumed before creation
PR or sprint: repository bootstrap
Lane: GitHub repository creation, local checkout, verification, and initial operational handoff
Owned scope: only the requested new GitHub repository and its selected local destination or source

Forbidden scope: credentials, displayed tokens, unrelated repositories, destructive overwrite, force-push, repository deletion, organization/team changes not explicitly requested, automatic public visibility, releases, deployment, or production data
Expected artifacts: remote repository URL, local root, origin URL, visibility evidence, initial branch/commit evidence when requested, command log, and exact next command
DIRECTORY GATE

1. A new repository may not have a local Git root yet. Resolve and verify the intended parent directory for clone mode, or the exact existing Git root for publish-existing mode, before any creation command.
2. The first executable command must enter the verified parent or source path with Set-Location -LiteralPath for PowerShell or cd -- for Bash.

3. In publish-existing mode, immediately run git rev-parse --show-toplevel, git status --short, git branch --show-current, git log --oneline --decorate -5, and git remote -v. Refuse the wrong root, detached or ambiguous history, unowned dirty work, or an existing conflicting origin.
4. In clone-new mode, verify that the intended child directory does not already contain unknown work. Never delete or overwrite it automatically.
PRECONDITIONS
1. Run git --version and gh --version.
2. Run gh auth status --active --hostname github.com. Never use --show-token, print environment tokens, request credentials in chat, or automate gh auth login.

3. Resolve the active account and confirm that xyz_owner is the intended user or organization. An authentication, owner, permission, network, or policy problem is a blocker, not permission to guess another owner.
4. Require an explicit repository name and one explicit visibility flag: --private, --public, or --internal. Do not default to public.

5. Check whether xyz_owner/xyz_repo_name already exists with gh repo view and classify success, confirmed not-found, authentication failure, permission failure, and network failure separately. Existing repository is a stop condition unless the task explicitly changes to clone or inspect it.
6. Validate optional license and gitignore names through GitHub CLI or repository evidence before using them. Omit unspecified bootstrap flags rather than guessing.
CREATION MODE A — NEW REMOTE AND LOCAL CLONE
Use a reviewable non-interactive command shaped as:
gh repo create "xyz_owner/xyz_repo_name" --private --description "xyz_description" --clone
Replace --private only with the explicitly selected visibility. Add --add-readme, --license, --gitignore, --template, --disable-issues, or --disable-wiki only when explicitly selected and validated.

After creation, enter the newly created child directory and verify git rev-parse --show-toplevel, git remote get-url origin, git branch --show-current, git log --oneline --decorate -5, and git status --short.
CREATION MODE B — PUBLISH AN EXISTING LOCAL REPOSITORY
Use only when the operator explicitly selected the verified existing repository and approved publishing its current history.
Use a reviewable command shaped as:
gh repo create "xyz_owner/xyz_repo_name" --private --description "xyz_description" --source "<verified-root>" --remote origin --push

Omit --push when the operator has not approved pushing existing commits. Refuse an unexpected existing remote, secret or private generated artifacts, detached HEAD, unreviewed history, or a dirty worktree outside owned scope.
VERIFICATION
1. Run gh repo view "xyz_owner/xyz_repo_name" --json nameWithOwner,url,visibility,defaultBranchRef.
2. From the verified local root, run git remote get-url origin, git branch --show-current, git log --oneline --decorate -5, and git status --short.
3. Confirm that the remote owner/name and visibility exactly match the request and that the local origin points to that repository.
4. When an initial push was requested, confirm the remote branch contains the expected commit. Do not infer push success from local state alone.
FINAL RESPONSE

Report repo, verified parent and final local root, active GitHub account without token material, exact creation mode and command, selected visibility and bootstrap flags, remote URL, origin URL, branch and commit evidence, files created, validation results, skipped checks, blockers, proof level, proof ceiling, final Git state, and one exact directory-safe next command.
Do not claim creation, clone, commit, push, or remote visibility without direct CLI evidence.
